methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $10.32B

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $3.1B

data.narrative:
Ultima Genomics is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $700M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M, Series B).

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C progression, yielding $1.2B. Peer-median valuation across the four same-sector comparables registers $3.5B, with a range from $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 6.9x) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures. These two methods triangulate to a range of $1.0B to $4.0B, with a median of $2.3B.

Key uncertainties center on revenue traction and technical differentiation within a dispersed peer set. The $3.0B spread between methods reflects wide heterogeneity in recent biotech valuations at this stage, particularly the $10.3B outlier (Colossal). The median of $2.3B balances stage-based fundamentals against peer positioning but remains sensitive to undisclosed operational and commercial metrics.
